text.content:
Bryn Shander

Friendliness ❄❄❄  |  Services ❄❄❄  |  Comfort ❄❄❄

The first stop for most visitors to Icewind Dale, Bryn Shander is a walled town perched atop a cold, lonely, wind-lashed hill. Bright lanterns suspended over narrow streets twist in the wind. The walls stand 30 feet high — two concentric rings of upright wooden poles filled with dirt and rubble, with a 15-foot hinged gate that is kept closed whenever it's dark outside (which is most of the time).

Auril's unending winter has reduced trade and visitors, eating away at the locals' sense of humor and goodwill. Still, there is no safer place in Icewind Dale to spend coin or spend the night.

At a Glance

  • Population: 1,200
  • Leaders: Speaker Duvessa Shane (lawful good human noble) — young but capable, head of the Council of Speakers. Sheriff Markham Southwell (lawful good human veteran) — commands the militia.
  • Militia: Up to 250 soldiers (tribal warrior) and 20 veterans
  • Heraldry: Black antlers above a vertical shaft of golden wheat on a white field
  • Sacrifice to Auril: Humanoid — lottery held the afternoon before the new moon; the unlucky soul is stripped bare and sent into the tundra
  • Rivalries: None
  • Available Quest: Foaming Mugs

Overland Travel from Bryn Shander

DestinationTravel Time (on foot)
Caer-Dineval10½ hours
Easthaven7½ hours
Good Mead6 hours
Targos2 hours
Termalaine6 hours

Mounts and dogsleds reduce travel time by up to 50%.

Locations in Bryn Shander

Blackiron Blades (Smithy and Outfitter)

Garn the Hammer (lawful good shield dwarf commoner) manufactures the cheapest blades in Ten-Towns — quality so uninspiring that local jokes about hapless newcomers always end with "an' 'e was carrying a Blackiron blade, to boot!" His sister Elza (lawful good shield dwarf commoner) sells adventuring supplies: rations, cold weather gear, ice picks, snowshoes.

House of the Morninglord (Shrine of Amaunator)

Located near the northeast market square. Run by Mishann (neutral good human priest), a retired adventurer who aspires to see the sun return. She calls her god "the Morninglord" — note: she considers Lathander a usurper and gets irritated if the two are confused.

Copper Knobberknocker (chaotic good rock gnome acolyte of Lathander) rents Mishann's attic and helps with chores despite constant bickering. He wears a fuzzy self-made suit that makes him look like a teddy bear. Copper is worried about his friend Macreadus at the Black Cabin in the wilderness — Macreadus is trying to build a device to end the Frostmaiden's winter. If the characters pass through that area, Copper asks them to check in.

The Northlook (Inn and Tavern)

The establishment most frequented by mercenaries and adventurers — rowdiest and most dangerous place to stay, but the best spot in Ten-Towns to hear leads on profitable ventures. Proprietor: Scramsax (neutral human veteran), a retired sellsword who extends credit to adventurers between jobs but presents an interest-inflated bill when they next get paid. He still knows how to handle a blade.

Ol' Bitey: A stuffed, mounted knucklehead trout above the common room hearth. A prankster wizard cast a spell on it years ago — it snaps at any creature within 5 feet, and occasionally sings:
"There's a place I like to go / Farther up the river's flow; / Where it is, I do not know; / Must be under all that snow."

The ring from the Ring Hunter character secret may be lodged in Ol' Bitey's throat (Scramsax won't allow anyone to handle it without significant compensation).

Roll on the Ten-Towns Rumors table for gossip heard here.

The Stables

Houses sled dogs and axe beaks for hire or purchase. Dogsleds can be rented here.

Council Hall

Where the Council of Speakers meets when called by Speaker Duvessa Shane. Meetings are infrequent, called only for matters of shared interest or to settle disputes.
